comments.comment:
You've put in a solid effort on this assignment, and I appreciate your engagement with the questions! I noticed you tried to answer each one, which shows your willingness to participate. For example, your response about Monroe's message—'He said stay out of our hemisphere and we will stay out of yours.'—captures the essence of the Monroe Doctrine, but it could be clearer with a bit more context.

However, I want to encourage you to focus on providing complete answers. You left the summary question blank, which is a significant part of the assignment. Summarizing the Monroe Doctrine and its importance would help you consolidate your understanding of the topic. Remember, each question is an opportunity to show what you know!

Additionally, your writing could benefit from more clarity. For example, in your response about why the era of colonization was over, you wrote, 'To grow bigger and have more people.' This could be expanded to explain that the Americas were already independent and that further colonization would be met with resistance.

Keep up the effort! You're on the right track, and I can see that you have a good understanding of the material. With a bit more focus on clarity and completeness, I know you can improve your scores. I believe in your ability to connect these ideas and express them clearly. Let's keep building on your strengths and improving together!

Great job on this assignment! You clearly understood President Monroe's main message, stating that 'European nations should not try to take control of any country in the Western Hemisphere.' This shows a solid grasp of the Monroe Doctrine's purpose. I also appreciated your summary about its importance, especially when you mentioned that it was an attempt for peace and protection during a time of growing tensions.

However, I noticed a few areas where you could expand your answers for even better clarity. For example, when you mentioned that European interference 'would lead to aggression,' it would be helpful to explain what kind of aggression you were referring to. Adding a bit more detail can really strengthen your responses.

Also, in your answer about how the Monroe Doctrine showed U.S. power, you mentioned wanting to 'grow bigger and to make America stronger.' It would be great to connect this idea to specific examples of how this doctrine influenced U.S. actions in the future. Keep up the thoughtful work, and I look forward to seeing how you can build on these ideas in your next assignment!

You're making solid progress, especially with your understanding of historical concepts. Keep pushing yourself to elaborate more in your writing, and you'll continue to improve!

You've done a great job tackling the questions about the Monroe Doctrine! I really liked your summary where you mentioned 'interfering with countries in North and South America, declaring the Western Hemisphere closed to new European control.' This shows you understand the main idea of the doctrine well. Keep up that good work!

I noticed that you attempted all the questions, which is fantastic! However, some of your answers could be clearer. For example, in your response about why the era of colonization was over, you wrote 'He did not what Europe making more colonies in the United state of America.' It would be clearer to say something like 'He believed that Europe should stop making more colonies in the United States.' This will help you express your ideas more effectively.

Your effort is commendable, and I can see that you're trying to engage with the material. Just remember to focus on clarity in your writing. For instance, when discussing why European nations didn't take the Monroe Doctrine seriously, you could elaborate a bit more on their initial perceptions. This will strengthen your responses.

Overall, you're making great strides! Your engagement and willingness to answer all questions is a real strength. Keep working on expressing your ideas clearly, and I'm confident you'll continue to improve. Great job on this assignment!
